title: The Ethnic Tip

jokes.text: My guess is you don't have the right stuff.
jokes.setup: Kellogg asks what Donnie has that he doesn't have
jokes.punchline: Carlton references New Kids on the Block song 'The Right Stuff'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay callback to the pop culture reference. Clean setup/payoff with good economy.

jokes.text: Sorry about that, Coach, but... I thought I'd run a few laps before class. Help out the old basketball team.
jokes.setup: Will is late to class and needs an excuse
jokes.punchline: He claims tardiness was due to voluntary extra basketball practice
jokes.explanation: Classic Will smooth-talking his way out of trouble. Good character integration but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Professor Smiley. And for that little slip of the tongue, 20 laps.
jokes.setup: Student calls him 'Coach' instead of 'Professor'
jokes.punchline: Petty punishment for minor infraction establishes his character
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - shows Smiley's petty authoritarianism. Sets up his character for the episode.

jokes.text: I think I aced this one. How about you, Will? I think I did just fine.
jokes.setup: Carlton confidently thinks he aced the test, Will claims he did fine
jokes.punchline: The irony that Will is about to reveal a terrible grade
jokes.explanation: Sets up dramatic irony - audience will soon learn Will failed. Basic but functional setup.

jokes.text: And the little smiley face you use to dot your 'I'... shows that you got way too much time on your hands.
jokes.setup: Will is doing handwriting analysis for Ashley
jokes.punchline: Delivers mock-serious analysis that's actually just an insult
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the handwriting analysis bit. Will's street-smart personality shines through the fake mysticism.

jokes.text: Handsome movie star, who will only be happy... if you spend obscene amounts of money.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks who her handwriting says she'll marry
jokes.punchline: Will gives a cynical prediction about her wealthy lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about the Banks family's wealth and Ashley's privileged perspective. Good character work.

jokes.text: One look at my signature and he deduced that I was valiant... ingenious, and have the strength of 10.
jokes.setup: Carlton describes what Will supposedly deduced from his handwriting
jokes.punchline: Overly grandiose self-assessment reveals Carlton's ego
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - his inflated self-image comes through. The 'strength of 10' is particularly absurd for Carlton's physique.

jokes.text: No, Uncle Phil. You deserve a fresh piece of paper.
jokes.setup: Phil asks where to sign for handwriting analysis
jokes.punchline: Will implies Phil's signature is so large it needs its own paper
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Phil's size translated to his signature. Good economy and character awareness.

jokes.text: Getting you and Mom to sign his 'Hall of Shame' History exam.
jokes.setup: Ashley mentions the handwriting analysis is sad
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals Will's real purpose - getting parents to sign failed test
jokes.explanation: Great reveal that recontextualizes the entire handwriting scene. Good dramatic structure and Carlton being a tattletale.

jokes.text: Sorry, Will.
jokes.setup: Will's scheme has been exposed
jokes.punchline: Ashley's simple, flat apology for ruining his con
jokes.explanation: Nice understated moment. Ashley's innocent directness contrasts with Will's scheming.

jokes.text: You will? You will? You will?
jokes.setup: Vivian volunteers to go to the parent-teacher meeting
jokes.punchline: Escalating repetition suggests mounting panic from Will and Carlton
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat - the escalating panic is visual. Sets up their anxiety about Vivian getting involved.

jokes.text: This should certainly be fly.
jokes.setup: Will is about to present his case
jokes.punchline: Preppy headmaster uses outdated slang awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Classic wealthy white character trying to use Black slang. Good cringe comedy through character contrast.

jokes.text: The last movie I saw was Dead Poets Society.
jokes.setup: Will references Spike Lee's 'Do the Right Thing'
jokes.punchline: Armstrong's cultural disconnect shown through his movie preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work - shows the cultural gap between Will and the prep school world. Good use of movie references.

jokes.text: With the right headmaster... that would have been a completely different story.
jokes.setup: Armstrong mentions Dead Poets Society
jokes.punchline: Implies he would have handled the teacher situation differently (more harshly)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about his authoritarian tendencies. Self-aware joke about being the antagonist headmaster.

jokes.text: I don't know a lick more than what's in this book. So forget it.
jokes.setup: Smiley makes reasonable points about curriculum limitations
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about his own ignorance and unwillingness to learn
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures lazy teacher mentality. The blunt honesty is both refreshing and damning.

jokes.text: Article 4, Section 9. / Where'd you get ahold of that book?
jokes.setup: Armstrong cites specific school rule requiring 150 signatures
jokes.punchline: Phil's surprise that Armstrong actually knows the rulebook
jokes.explanation: Implies Armstrong usually doesn't know school policy. Phil's shocked reaction suggests this is out of character for the headmaster.

jokes.text: A month off? Armstrong, I think the kid's got something here.
jokes.setup: Will suggests getting someone to replace Smiley for a month
jokes.punchline: Smiley immediately supports the idea for selfish reasons
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Smiley's immediate self-interest overrides any educational concern.

jokes.text: You're looking at her. See you in a month.
jokes.setup: Armstrong describes the qualifications needed for the replacement teacher
jokes.punchline: Vivian reveals she meets all qualifications and takes the job immediately
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot twist with great economy. Vivian's confidence and the 'see you in a month' is perfect.

jokes.text: What, are you tripping? How do you figure that? Because the teacher's my mommy.
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton argue about who will be the star of the class
jokes.punchline: Carlton's childish logic and word choice
jokes.explanation: Carlton's use of 'mommy' is perfectly childish. Shows his assumption that family connection equals advantage.

jokes.text: What did you call me, Mr. Banks? / What I always call you: 'Mom.' / My name is not 'Mom.' My name is Professor Banks.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to use familiarity to his advantage in class
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv immediately shuts down any special treatment
jokes.explanation: Establishes Aunt Viv's professional standards. Carlton's plan backfires immediately. The formality creates distance and authority.

jokes.text: That was very well put, Mr. Smith, but you're wrong.
jokes.setup: Will gives confident explanation about spirituals
jokes.punchline: His own aunt publicly corrects him in class
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - Will thought he'd coast but gets shut down immediately. Good setup for his academic struggles.

jokes.text: That covers about 250 years, Mr. Smith. Care to narrow it down a bit?
jokes.setup: Will answers 'during slavery' as the timeframe
jokes.punchline: Vivian's sarcastic response about the vagueness
jokes.explanation: Good academic humor - exposes Will's lack of specificity. Vivian's teacher voice is perfect.

jokes.text: That's what I meant.
jokes.setup: Vivian gives detailed correct explanation
jokes.punchline: Will's weak attempt to save face
jokes.explanation: Classic student trying to save face after being corrected. Simple but effective cringe moment.

jokes.text: Congratulations, Will. What an honor.
jokes.setup: Will gets assigned the first big report
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic congratulations
jokes.explanation: Carlton enjoying Will's predicament. Basic but character-consistent sarcasm.

jokes.text: This course was a brilliant idea. She's your mom!
jokes.setup: Both boys realize they're getting extra work
jokes.punchline: The irony that their family connection makes things worse, not better
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Carlton's earlier confidence. The family dynamic backfiring is well-executed.

jokes.text: He used to say, 'Extra work for you... means extra work for me.'
jokes.setup: Discussing how Professor Smiley never gave extra assignments
jokes.punchline: Reveals Smiley's lazy teaching philosophy
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Smiley's established character. Reveals more about his teaching style.

jokes.text: I'm Carlton Banks. That's right, the Carlton Banks.
jokes.setup: Carlton introducing himself to girls
jokes.punchline: Acts like he's a celebrity when he's clearly not
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - his inflated self-importance reaches absurd levels. The phrase 'the Carlton Banks' is perfectly delusional.

jokes.text: Excuse me, miss. Maybe you can help me out here. I've lost the feeling in my lips.
jokes.setup: Carlton approaches an attractive girl with a pickup line
jokes.punchline: Uses the world's worst/most awkward pickup line
jokes.explanation: Peak Carlton awkwardness. The line makes no sense and is incredibly inappropriate. Perfect character moment of social ineptitude.

jokes.text: It's right around the corner from the Pencil School?
jokes.setup: Vivian asks what he learned about the Penn School
jokes.punchline: Will's weak wordplay shows he wasn't listening
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that shows Will's inattention. The joke is intentionally bad to show his academic struggles.

jokes.text: Look at you. Honestly, Will... those eyebrows!
jokes.setup: Vivian switches to mom mode
jokes.punchline: Random criticism about Will's appearance
jokes.explanation: Shows Vivian switching between teacher and aunt roles. The specificity of 'eyebrows' is funny.

jokes.text: The tall one really liked me.
jokes.setup: Carlton was trying to impress girls earlier
jokes.punchline: His delusional confidence despite obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his earlier scene. Carlton's self-delusion is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Because I've always been partial to food and shelter.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks why Geoffrey has worked his entire life
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry response about basic survival needs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey - dry British wit contrasting with Hilary's privileged obliviousness. Great economy and delivery.

jokes.text: It's like, I mean... Leave me alone! You're hired.
jokes.setup: Hilary struggles to answer basic interview questions
jokes.punchline: Her complete breakdown followed by Geoffrey's sarcastic 'hiring'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Hilary's incompetence. The 'You're hired' is perfectly timed sarcasm from Geoffrey.

jokes.text: You're hired.
jokes.setup: Hilary just had a complete verbal meltdown about her qualifications
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey deadpan declares she's hired despite the disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery after Hilary's meltdown. The absurdity of hiring someone after that performance is peak Geoffrey humor.

jokes.text: My body needs a certain amount of microwaves.
jokes.setup: Will explains his TV withdrawal symptoms
jokes.punchline: Absurd medical justification using wrong terminology
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist logic. The confusion of 'microwaves' (radiation) with TV waves is clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Last night, I had a dream that I went to the prom with Harriet Tubman.
jokes.setup: Will explains how the history class is affecting them
jokes.punchline: Surreal dream mixing teenage concerns with historical figures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image. The juxtaposition of prom (teenage normalcy) with Harriet Tubman is wonderfully surreal and specific.

jokes.text: Umoja. What? Umoja. It's Swahili for 'unity.' Dope, huh?
jokes.setup: Carlton randomly uses a Swahili word
jokes.punchline: His pride in this shallow cultural knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - trying to show cultural awareness in the most superficial way possible. The pride in his 'dope' is cringe-perfect.

jokes.text: Shoot me.
jokes.setup: Students want Vivian to become permanent teacher
jokes.punchline: Carlton's quiet despair at the prospect
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that capture Carlton's complete dread. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: After you, Will. / No, you go ahead first. / I insist. / She's your mom. / This class was your idea.
jokes.setup: The boys are asked to sign the petition to make Aunt Viv permanent
jokes.punchline: Both try to avoid signing by being overly polite to each other
jokes.explanation: Classic avoidance comedy. Both boys desperately trying not to be the one to commit to more torture. The politeness masks panic.

jokes.text: I hope you don't take this the wrong way... but you made our lives a living nightmare.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to diplomatically criticize his mother
jokes.punchline: The extreme language 'living nightmare' after the polite preface
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Carlton's attempted politeness and his actual harsh complaint. The escalation works well.

jokes.text: This whole ugly episode... it's not going to affect my grade, will it?
jokes.setup: Carlton has just criticized his mother's teaching
jokes.punchline: Immediately worries about academic consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - even when confronting his mother, he's worried about his GPA. Shows his priorities clearly.

jokes.text: And you're very beautiful, Mommy.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she's a tough grader
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately resorts to childish flattery
jokes.explanation: Carlton's immediate switch to sucking up. The 'Mommy' makes it particularly childish and desperate.

jokes.text: And you're very beautiful, Mommy.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she's a tough grader
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately tries to butter her up with childish flattery
jokes.explanation: Carlton's transparent attempt at grade manipulation through flattery

jokes.text: Aunt Viv, I read The Autobiography of Malcolm X three times.
jokes.setup: Vivian questions Will's commitment to Black history
jokes.punchline: Will's defense focuses on just one book, showing limited engagement
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's shallow engagement with the subject. One book repeated doesn't equal deep knowledge.

tmdb_overview: Will proposes a black history class be instituted at Bel-Air Academy, and gets a surprise when Aunt Viv arrives to teach it.
